feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Does Sap Cloud Identity Services (CIS) - IAS/IPS support position based security?
entry.content:
I am interested to know if the new age SAP Cloud Identity solutions suppor the position based security for S/4HANA (HCM on S/4HANA)?
I could not find any mention of this anywhere on sap help documentations.

entry.title: Joule in Ariba Guided Buying: “Open in App” fails – sap.ushell.Container undefined, Fiori 3 CSS
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m seeing an issue with Joule “Open in App” in SAP Ariba Guided Buying and wanted to check if anyone has faced this before.

What I’m doing:
From Joule, I see a list of recent Purchase Requisitions. When I click Open in App on a PR, it should take me to the full Guided Buying PR screen.

What happens instead:
I get an error saying “The application could not be opened”, and nothing opens.

What I noticed while checking in browser tools:

  • Console shows messages like:

    • Unable to handle navigation with payload

    • No sap.ushell Container found

  • A CSS file does not load:
    /gb/sap_fiori_3_fonts.css
    This request returns 500 Internal Server Error and JSON instead of CSS.

    My questions:

    • Is this a known Guided Buying / Joule issue?

    • Does “Open in App” require any specific tenant or shell configuration?

    • Has anyone seen this issue and found a fix, or is raising an SAP ticket the only option?

      I have Added the screenshots in the following

       

    Any pointers would be really helpful.
    Thanks in advance!

entry.title: IAG–S/4HANA User ID Mapping Requirement
entry.content:
Hi

I have requirement regarding an integration involving SAP Identity Access Governance (IAG), SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS), and our on-premise SAP S/4HANA system.

We are currently provisioning users from Microsoft Entra ID into SAP Cloud Identity Services, where users will create and assigned P-User IDs. Once the User IDs created in CIS, We are maintaining “Login Name” as our required naming convention (XXYYZZNN) for S/4 HANA system.

As per SAP Recommendation we are creating user IDs as P user IDs in BTP-IAG Subaccount. However, our S/4HANA system requires user IDs to follow a specific internal naming convention (XXYYZZNN). We need guidance and confirmation on the recommended approach to ensure that:

1.SAP IAG provisions users into S/4HANA with our required naming convention (XXYYZZNN) instead of the IAS P-User ID.

2. Attribute mapping and transformation rules between IAS, IPS, IAG, and S/4HANA. We would appreciate assistance advising on the correct configuration steps for attribute mapping and user ID transformation within IAG.

entry.title: How to enable Joule for the SAC Tenant Owner in S4 public cloud?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I'm looking for a solution of the problem I'm currently facing.

I went through the whole Blog and got it done to setup the connection between SAC and Joule in S4 Public Cloud.... but only for a regular user (with BI Admin and Admin role), but not for the SAC tenant Owner (who has no roles).

The first time, this was the result of reading the users, like described in step 4.5 Schedule User and Group Provisioning (optional)

Not sure what the real root cause was... I had the feeling that only users with roles are processed, so I put myself (the owner) into a Team and assigned the Admin roles to the team.

In the second try, it looks like also my user got processed:

However, in S4 public Cloud, when I try to get answer from a SAC model by Joule, I'm asked if my question is related to Ledger 0L and which Company Code ... and that get the information that Joule would not be able to access any SAC tenant.

In contrast to that, my colleague is able to get analytical answers from SAC.... so the whole setup basically works!

Did anybody experience the same issue and did find a solution?

Addition: No, I have no API Access Users in my tenant. This is a fresh TDD SAC, where only 2 Users and 1 Team exists.

Thanks a lot, Martin

entry.title: Performing a OAUTH token exchange from a PKCE generated token on client x to another client y
entry.content:
I have a set of APIs from a CAP application which are available behind the AppRouter. I want to access these APIs from an application which will be using PKCE to authenticate.

The identity provider for CAP application with the APIs is based on IAS authentication as a private client. The PKCE application is also based on IAS authentication but is a public client to use the PKCE authentication flow.

All of the existing SAP documentation regarding the token exchange process refers to the exchange with XSUAA involved but I am not using XSUAA and will only be using IAS for authentication.

I am attempting to use a 'Destination' with an authentication of type 'OAuth2UserTokenExchange':

When I initiate a call via the destination providing the PKCE token with the intent of accessing the CAP application APIs, I am receiving the error:

Error: The destination tried to provide authorization tokens but failed in all cases. This is most likely due to misconfiguration. 
Retrieval of OAuthToken failed due to: Unable to fetch refresh token from the specified token service URL. Response was: Bad credentials

I have confirmed I have provided the correct client ID and Secret as extracted from the 'Secret' I created in Trust -> Application APIs for the registered client for the CAP application via IAS.

Question:

1. Is it feasible to perform a token exchange from IAS to IAS on different clients?

2. Is this destination configuration correct?

3. Is this the correct location to get the client ID and client secret?

entry.title: No Access to SAP Cloud CBC Demo
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have purchased the SAP Cloud ERP Public Edition demo system for 3 months (Cloud test, demo, and development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, one-system landscape):
https://www.sap.com/germany/products/erp/s4hana.html

We received the Identity Authentication Service (IAS) tenant from SAP, and we can log in there and create users and administrators. We can also access the Cloud ERP Public Edition demo system and create users, roles, etc.

However, we cannot access the Central Business Configuration (CBC) system. Even though we have assigned the administrators in IAS with all CBC roles, when we open the CBC link we receive the error message “Unauthorized.”

On the page “Cloud Identity Services – Tenants”, we noticed that SAP has automatically bundled the CBC demo system with an old IPS tenant from 2016.

 

Because of this, we also do not have the following option available in IAS?
Under Administrator → “Manage Identity Provisioning.”

 

 

 

 

Although we already have all administrator roles and groups in BTP/ IAS/IPS, we cannot see this setting. Therefore, we cannot schedule a provisioning job from IAS to CBC.

According to SAP documentation, IPS tenants created before 2022 are configured with SAP BTP Neo. Is that true?

What should we do in this case?
Is there any way for us to access our CBC demo system?
Or does SAP need to provide us with a new tenant?

 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: XSUAA not getting the subject name identifier as ID
entry.content:
Hi,

We're trying to change `req.user.id` used from xsuaa to the User ID of IAS instead of the email.

BTP is connected to IAS with OpenID Connect (so "Default Name ID Format = Unspecified" as with SAML is not available). 

In the IAS logs the sub is changed to the expected value `jwtPayload="{"sub":"`.

However the req.user.id we get in CAP / JWT token remains the email.

Not sure if it's related, but adding a custom atribute in `xs-security.json` and in IAS attributes also isn't reflected in `req.user.attr`. (it only shows the default value configured in `xs-security.json`)

Anyone have some insights on how to get the id ?

entry.title: " &prompt=none" response back to customer's IDP
entry.content:
We are implementing IAS for a customer and the customer is requiring that the parameter "prompt=none" be included in the OIDC authorization request to their IdP.

Prompt is typically an optional parameter. Without it, the client system should detect whether the user already has a valid login session and only prompt for authentication if one does not exist. Which is the most secure option and in my experience IAS is always configured with the strictest options.

IAS does not provide a way to alter or append parameters in the OIDC request it sends to the client IdP.

Thoughts on how to pass the '&prompt=none' string back to customer's idp?

entry.title: I have no subscription option for Workzone
entry.content:
Hello,

I can only create an instance for SAP Build Workzone in my subaccount (not trial), but there is no subscription option:


I have all entitles for Workzone in my global account:

I have also subscribed to the Cloud Identity Service, but in the Trust Configuration, I have a different tenant for the Cloud Identity Service than the one in my subscriptions. Could this be the source of my problem with the missing Workzone subscription option?

Cloud Identity Service-Tenant in my Thrust:

Cloud-Identity-Service-Tenant in "Instances and Subscriptions":

I would be thankful if somebody can help me.

Best regards
Timur

entry.title: Cannot subscribe to Cloud Identity Service
entry.content:
I tried to subscribe to the Cloud Identity Service in multiple SAP BTP subaccounts, but none of them worked. I also tried using a new email to create a new BTP trial account, but I still couldn’t subscribe to Cloud Identity. I attempted to delete and subscribe again, but that didn’t work either. Switching between the TEST and PRODUCTIVE parameters also had no effect.

I checked the entitlements, and Cloud Identity is included. Also tried remove the entitlement and add it again, result still the same

Has anyone faced a similar issue? Please help me with this.

entry.title: IAS activation email not triggered after 2nd rehire check
entry.content:
Hello all, 

Has anyone come across IAS issues with welcome email not being triggered after 2nd rehire check and ONB is restarted for the new hire? The SF standard ONB emails get triggered, but there is no new IAS welcome email to activate their account after the rehire match. As per this KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/3585126/E, there should be a 2nd activation email or password reset email of some sort triggered from IAS, but this isn't happening for the client.

I have a High priority ticket with SAP and it's been with their engineering team for a week now with no update, hoping someone here has an answer or can at least point me in the right direction.

Thank you!

entry.title: Adding Learning Content Provider as Application in IAS
entry.content:
Hello,

We are in the process of setting up a new learning content provider in our SF LMS system.  For various reasons for authentication we have been told that the best route to take would be to set the learning content provider up as an application in IAS. 

We are an SSO environment and have SSO configured in IAS.  By setting up the Learning Content Provider in IAS, it would use IAS for authentication when a person is accessing a course.  

I logged a ticket with SAP and I was given this KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0002677737 and told that this would be the correct path to take and that we do not need to configure it in IAS. 

I went back to the Learning Content Provider with this information. They have said that while this works, it is very basic and will only send the userID and not information like names and that other customers of theirs have set them up in IAS. 

Just curious if anyone has had this scenario before and what approach they took? 

Kind regards,
Helen

entry.title: Application Frontend Service app with IAS leads to 401
entry.content:
We're building an integration monitoring application on SAP BTP using SAP CAP (Node.js) as the backend and a SAPUI5 Fiori Elements app as the frontend. For deployment, we chose the Application Frontend Service (app-front) as a managed approuter — instead of deploying and maintaining our own approuter module.

Is there any example of Application Frontend Service with IAS or also with your own CAP backend? I don't know what is broken.

For authentication, we deliberately went with IAS (Identity Authentication Service) only — no XSUAA.

This mta.yaml let's you deploy, but will end up with 401 Unauthorized calls to the backend in the browser logs of the deployed frontend app:

 

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: cpi-monitoring
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
  - name: cpi-monitoring-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    properties:
      AMS_DCL_ROOT: ams/dcl
    requires:
      - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres
      - name: cpi-monitoring-ias
        parameters:
          config:
            app-identifier: srv
            credential-type: X509_GENERATED
    provides:
      - name: srv-api
        properties:
          srv-cert-url: ${protocol}://${default-host}.cert.${default-domain}
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      instances: 1
      routes:
        - route: ${default-url}
        - route: ${default-host}.cert.${default-domain}
    build-parameters:
      builder: npm-ci
    deployed-after:
      - cpi-monitoring-ams-policies-deployer
  - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres-deployer
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/pg
    requires:
      - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      no-route: true
      no-start: true
      tasks:
        - command: npm start
          name: deploy-to-postgresql
  - name: cpi-monitoring-ams-policies-deployer
    type: javascript.nodejs
    path: gen/policies
    requires:
      - name: cpi-monitoring-ias
        parameters:
          config:
            app-identifier: ams-policy-deployer
            credential-type: X509_GENERATED
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      no-route: true
      no-start: true
      tasks:
        - command: npm start
          memory: 512M
          name: deploy-dcl
  # - name: cpi-monitoring-app-content # for Work Zone deployment
  #   type: com.sap.application.content
  #   path: .
  #   requires:
  #     - name: cpi-monitoring-repo-host
  #       parameters:
  #         content-target: true
  #   build-parameters:
  #     build-result: resources
  #     requires:
  #       - artifacts:
  #           - monitoring.zip
  #         name: monitoring
  #         target-path: resources/
  # - name: monitoring
  #   type: html5
  #   path: app/monitoring
  #   build-parameters:
  #     build-result: dist
  #     builder: custom
  #     commands:
  #       - npm install
  #       - npm run build:cf
  #       - npm ci
  #       - npm run build
  #     supported-platforms:
  #       []

  - name: cpi-monitoring-app-deployer
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - monitoring.zip
          name: cpimonitoringmonitoring
          target-path: resources/
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
      - name: cpi-monitoring-app-front
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    parameters:
      config:
        destinations:
          - name: monitoring
            url: ~{srv-api/srv-url}
            forwardAuthToken: true
            forwardAuthCertificates: true
          - name: ui5
            url: https://ui5.sap.com

  - name: cpimonitoringmonitoring
    type: html5
    path: app/monitoring
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms: []

resources:
  - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: postgresql-db
      service-plan: free
  - name: cpi-monitoring-ias
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        authorization:
          enabled: true
        display-name: cpi-monitoring
        oauth2-configuration:
          token-policy:
            access-token-format: jwt
        provided-apis:
          - description: API exposed by the application
            name: cpi-monitoring-ias-api
        xsuaa-cross-consumption: true
      service: identity
      service-name: cpi-monitoring-ias
      service-plan: application
  # - name: cpi-monitoring-repo-host # for Work Zone deployment
  #   type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  #   parameters:
  #     service: html5-apps-repo
  #     service-name: cpi-monitoring-html5-service
  #     service-plan: app-host
  - name: cpi-monitoring-destination-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: false
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-name: cpi-monitoring-destination-service
      service-plan: lite
  - name: cpi-monitoring-app-front
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: app-front
      service-plan: developer
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm ci
        - npx cds build --production

 

 

Destination name mismatch (404 on OData calls)

xs-app.json routes OData requests to destination: "monitoring". We initially had the destination named srv-api in the app-deployer config. Result: every OData call returned 404 with a message like:

Error while retrieving destination monitoring from destination service. HTTP status code: 404

Fix was: rename the destination to monitoring — exactly matching xs-app.json.

401 Unauthorized from CAP backend

After fixing the 404, we got 401 from the backend on every OData request. The app-front debug logs showed:

Exchange token required = false

When I try to change things the deployment often fails like this:

[cpi-monitoring-destination-content] [ERROR] Unexpected error occurred

entry.title: Change User ID in SAP Cloud Identity Services
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

is there any chance to change the User ID of a User in SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication Service) afterwards? 

In UI it is not possible and as far as I can see it is also not possible using the SCIM API (Identity Directory API). But maybe there's someone who has an idea.

Could it be changed by SAP Support?

Any idea is much appreciated.

Best Regards

Tobi

entry.title: "organization name" "50"
entry.content: I understand that the “Organization Name” field in SuccessFactors is limited to 50 characters. However, I have not been able to locate any official documentation (manuals, help pages, data model specifications, etc.) that explicitly states this field length.Could you please provide the location of the SAP official document that specifies:"The maximum length of the Organization Name field is 50 characters"?A URL, document name, or instructions on where to find the relevant material would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Possible attributes for IDP Transformation setting
entry.content:
Hello,

is there any overview available to see what attribute/ values I can select for source system "SAP Application Server ABAP" and for target system "Identity Authentication".

I want to adjust the default transformation to our use and therefor it would be great to know what can be selected and what's the correct name.

 

Best regards

Max

entry.title: IASテナントをSubaccount配下で有効化する構成についてのご質問
entry.content:
いつもお世話になっております。

SAP BTP Identity Authentication Service（IAS）の構成に関して、
以下の点についてご教示いただきたく、ご連絡いたしました。

■ 質問内容
IASテナントをSAP BTPのSubaccount配下に配置（または紐づけ）して
有効化できる、という話を耳にしました。

従来は、IASテナントはグローバルに提供され、
Subaccountとは論理的に連携する形で利用するものと理解しておりますが、
Subaccount単位でIASテナントを有効化・管理できるような構成が可能なのでしょうか。

■ お伺いしたい点
・本構成が実際にサポートされているのか
・実現方法（設定イメージや前提条件の概要）
・当該構成を採用することによるメリット
　（例：運用管理、テナント分離、セキュリティ、ライフサイクル管理 等）

entry.title: SSO Migration from ADFS to Entra ID. IAS – Guidance Request
entry.content:
Hello,

We are planning to migrate our corporate Identity Provider from ADFS to Microsoft Entra ID for SSO authentication. SuccessFactors (including LMS), using IAS. At this stage, we are in the planning phase and would like to begin with the LMS Preview environment before proceeding to Production. 

System / Landscape:

  • Product: SAP SuccessFactors (Learning)
  • Identity Layer: SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS)
  • Current Corporate IdP: ADFS
  • Planning Corporate IdP: Microsoft Entra ID
  • Environments: LMS Preview (Test) and LMS Production

We would appreciate guidance on the recommended approach to configure Entra ID as a new Corporate IdP in IAS alongside the existing ADFS.

Thank you,

Serguei,

entry.title: IAS/IPS tenants they are Neo or CF
entry.content: We have a set of IAS/IPS tenants that we are not sure whether they are Neo or CF. Could you please indicate from the attached list which ones are CF?

entry.title: Banner Name appearing on mobile app
entry.content: We see that banner name in the banner card is appearing in the mobile app of SF. We wish to disable this. How do we do that?
